behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can occur at module handoff between HTTP raw-body capture and signature verifier, not only inside one function. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: a Unit test of `verifyHmac(rawBody, sig)` passes, but real route wiring passes parsed/normalized body bytes, causing incorrect auth behavior. REAL-INFRA/LLM/MULTI-STEP: no real OS/network requirement and no model dependency, so not above Integration. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text only requires `401` + no forward, not real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler through real app middleware stack in-process (raw body capture + auth + enqueue adapter) with a mismatched `X-Signature-256`; assert HTTP 401 and assert queue write function/store receives zero writes.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bug appears across handler/queue boundary (`await` vs fire-and-forget). ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: Unit test of helper method passes, but actual controller responds 200 before queue commit promise resolves due wiring mistake. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network/subprocess needed beyond local modules + local persistent store; no AI dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Use app handler + real local persistent queue module (e.g., sqlite/file-backed test instance). Delay queue commit completion deterministically; assert HTTP 200 is not observed until commit completes, and commit record exists when 200 is returned.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is orchestrational handoff between ingress handler and async processor. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: Unit test of processor enqueue call passes, but integrated handler accidentally awaits downstream worker promise and blocks response. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced in-process with controlled async dependencies; no need for real OS/network.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with downstream processor stub that intentionally blocks (unresolved promise) after enqueue accept; send request through in-process HTTP stack and assert response returns promptly while processor still unfinished.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: requirement is explicit wall-clock SLA under concurrency; scheduler/event-loop/socket behavior is part of failure boundary. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: Integration test with mocked timers or direct function invocation passes, but real server under concurrent slow work exceeds 3s due event-loop starvation/backpressure. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because no AI/ML step is referenced.
behaviors.test_description: Start real Node process and send concurrent real HTTP requests while earlier events trigger intentionally slow downstream work; measure wall-clock latency per request and assert acknowledgments remain <3.0s.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Failure is usually at dedupe state handoff between handler and persistent queue constraint/index. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: Unit test of `isDuplicate(id)` passes, but integrated path writes duplicate because uniqueness check and insert are split across modules/race window. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real network/subprocess; no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use handler + real local persistent queue store with duplicate-ID rule (unique key or idempotency table). Submit same event ID twice and assert only one persisted queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is translation of queue-capacity signal to HTTP contract across module boundary. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: Unit test with simplified mock error passes, but real queue module emits structured/full-state error variant that handler mis-maps to 500 without `Retry-After`. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no real OS/network dependency required to expose this mapping bug; no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with actual queue module configured to full state (or deterministic full condition) and assert response is 503 and includes valid `Retry-After` header.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets queue implementation + persistence backend interaction, not just pure algorithm. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE: Unit test of in-memory counter passes, but real persistent store allows overflow due transaction/isolation/config mismatch. REJECTION-GATE: rejected System because behavior text is about queue enforcement itself, not real network/subprocess/OS primitives beyond local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue backend in test mode with small capacity (e.g., 2), enqueue up to limit, then enqueue one more; assert explicit rejection and no extra persisted record.
